skill.name: methodology-extractor
skill.description: Extract and compare experimental protocols across multiple research papers for systematic review and reproducibility assessment.
skill.summary: Automated extraction of experimental methods sections from papers with protocol comparison across studies.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 42 static analysis findings are false positives. The 12 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings match on unrelated descriptive text where no crypto code exists. The 26 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Markdown code formatting delimiters, not shell execution (zero Ruby code in the skill). The 'path traversal' findings are security checklist items warning against traversal. The 'system reconnaissance' and 'hardcoded URL' findings also lack malicious context. The skill is a legitimate research methodology extraction tool with a simple Python script that does text parsing only.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - FALSE POSITIVE
high_findings.description: Scanner detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' on lines containing descriptive text, YAML frontmatter, and argparse setup. No cryptographic operations exist anywhere in this skill. All 5 locations are false positives from overly broad pattern matching on non-cryptographic text.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence - FALSE POSITIVE
high_findings.description: Scanner detected '../' strings in security checklist items at SKILL.md lines 114 and 117. These are checklist items documenting that path traversal SHOULD be prevented, not code performing path traversal. The actual Python script (main.py) has no path traversal logic.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution - FALSE POSITIVE
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected 26 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns. All are Markdown code formatting backticks around Python commands in documentation. There is zero Ruby code in this skill. The SKILL.md uses ```bash fenced code blocks and inline backticks for command examples like 'python scripts/main.py --help'. These are documentation patterns, not execution.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance - FALSE POSITIVE
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 'system reconnaissance' on lines describing workflow steps and output format. These are standard documentation phrases, not reconnaissance commands. No host discovery, port scanning, or network enumeration occurs in this skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL - FALSE POSITIVE
low_findings.description: Scanner detected a URL at SKILL.md line 7. This is the source repository URL in the skill metadata header, used for attribution and reference. The Python script (main.py) makes zero network calls. This is a documentation link, not a network operation.

content.user_title: Extract and Compare Research Methods Across Papers
content.value_statement: Researchers waste hours manually reading papers to compare experimental protocols. This skill automates method extraction and protocol comparison across multiple papers for faster systematic reviews.
actual_capabilities: Extract methods sections from academic paper text using keyword detection
Identify numbered and bulleted protocol steps across multiple papers
Compare protocols to find common and unique steps between studies
Generate structured comparison output showing step variations
Run in demo mode with sample data to verify functionality
Output comparison data in a human-readable formatted table
limitations: Requires paper text to be provided as input - does not fetch papers from databases or APIs
Methods section detection uses simple keyword matching, not natural language processing
Does not verify extracted method data for accuracy or completeness
Only handles plain text input formats, not PDF or image files directly

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Methods Comparison
use_cases.description: Extract and compare experimental protocols across 50 or more papers to identify common practices and methodological variations for meta-analysis.

use_cases.title: Lab Protocol Benchmarking
use_cases.description: Compare a specific experimental method like Western blot or qPCR across published studies to identify optimal conditions and parameter ranges.

use_cases.title: Reproducibility Assessment
use_cases.description: Analyze methodological consistency across multiple studies on the same topic to evaluate how well protocols are standardized and reported.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Method Extraction from One Paper
prompt_templates.prompt:
Extract the methods section from this paper and list all protocol steps found:

[Paste paper text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick extraction of methods from a single paper for initial review.

prompt_templates.title: Protocol Comparison Across Papers
prompt_templates.prompt:
Compare the experimental protocols across these papers. Identify steps that are common to all papers and steps that are unique to each paper.

Paper 1: [Paper 1 text]
Paper 2: [Paper 2 text]
Paper 3: [Paper 3 text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-study comparison to find consensus protocols and unique variations.

prompt_templates.title: Parameter Variation Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt:
For the method type '[method name]', extract and compare all numerical parameters across these papers. Create a table showing each parameter and its values per paper.

Papers: [list of paper texts]
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed quantitative comparison of specific method parameters across studies.

prompt_templates.title: Full Systematic Review Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt:
Process this batch of papers for a systematic review. For each paper: 1) Extract the methods section, 2) Identify all protocol steps, 3) Note any deviations from standard protocols. Then generate a comparison table showing common steps, unique steps, and parameter variations.

Papers: [paper texts]
Method type: [specific method]
Output format: structured comparison with assumptions noted
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end systematic review methodology extraction with quality documentation.

output_examples.input: Three papers describing Western blot protocols for protein detection in cell lysates.
output_examples.output: COMMON STEPS (across all 3 papers): Cell culture and treatment, Protein extraction and quantification, SDS-PAGE separation, Membrane transfer, Antibody incubation. VARIATIONS: Blocking conditions differ (5% milk vs 3% BSA), Wash buffer compositions vary (PBST vs TBST), Detection method (ECL vs colorimetric). UNIQUE STEPS: Paper 1 includes a stripping and re-probing step not present in others.

output_examples.input: Comparison of RNA extraction methods from 10 papers studying gene expression in plant tissues.
output_examples.output: Protocol comparison reveals 3 main RNA extraction methods used: TRIzol-based (6 papers), commercial column kit (3 papers), and CTAB-based (1 paper). Common steps across all methods: Tissue homogenization in liquid nitrogen, Phase separation, RNA precipitation, DNase treatment. Key parameter variations: Homogenization time (30 sec to 2 min), Centrifugation speed (12000g to 16000g), Elution volume (30 uL to 50 uL).

best_practices: Prepare paper texts in a consistent format before extraction, removing references sections and figure captions for cleaner methods detection.
Define the specific method type and parameters of interest before running the comparison to focus the analysis on relevant protocol steps.
Always review extracted protocol steps against the original papers to confirm completeness before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to evaluate research quality, validity, or statistical rigor - it only extracts and compares method descriptions.
Do not assume extracted methods are complete without human verification, as keyword-based detection may miss protocol steps described in non-standard formats.
Do not use this skill on papers outside your domain expertise without consulting a domain expert, as method terminology varies by field.

faq.question: What types of papers does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill works with plain text from any academic paper that has a clearly labeled methods or materials and methods section.

faq.question: Does this skill fetch papers from PubMed or other databases?
faq.answer: No. You must provide the paper text as input. The skill does not connect to any external databases or APIs.

faq.question: How accurate is the methods extraction?
faq.answer: The extraction uses keyword matching to find methods sections. Accuracy depends on consistent section formatting. Always verify results against the original papers.

faq.question: Can this skill compare methods from different research fields?
faq.answer: Yes, but comparison is most useful within the same field where protocols follow similar conventions and terminology.

faq.question: Does this skill work with PDF files?
faq.answer: No. This skill works with plain text input. You need to extract text from PDFs before using this skill.

faq.question: What method types can I search for?
faq.answer: Any method type described in the papers. Common examples include Western blot, qPCR, ELISA, immunohistochemistry, cell culture, and RNA extraction.
